item.title: 'Zeteo Is a Pathetic Fake News Blog,' Says an Angry Trump White House
item.description: ⚽️ The World Cup may be ending, but you can support your favorite independent journalism team – Team Zeteo! – year-round with our brand-new football/soccer jerseys! Visit our online store now to purchase them and all of our other Zeteo-branded merch. Paid subscribers get 20% off their orders! At…
item.author: "Zeteo | U.S. Politics"
item.content:encoded:
⚽️ The World Cup may be ending, but you can support your favorite independent journalism team – Team Zeteo! – year-round with our brand-new football/soccer jerseys! Visit our online store now to purchase them and all of our other Zeteo-branded merch. Paid subscribers get 20% off their orders!

At the beginning of this month, we pissed off the Trump White House.

Again.

We ran a story on how President Donald Trump is already planning to steal classified U.S. documents after he leaves office a second time – just like he did the first time. The West Wing got huffy about it.

“Zeteo is a pathetic fake news blog and their so-called sources have no idea what they’re talking about. Zeteo’s three readers already know this ‘publication’ is total fake news,” the White House told us in an email. No one signed their name. (If you read their statement closely, you’ll notice they did not deny any of our reporting… 🤣🤣🤣)

The federal government is waging a nonstop war on the First Amendment in ways that we haven’t seen in the United States in a long, long time. On top of that, senior White House officials constantly hurl childish taunts and bullshit smears at news organizations that shine a light on what this corrupt, out-of-his-mind president actually does.

Their enemies list includes Zeteo.

The Trump White House has publicly denounced us as “ terrorist sympathizers,” because we’ve reported on the

Top MAGA ally and the world’s richest man, Elon Musk, has been paying attention to what Mehdi and Zeteo have been doing too and… he doesn’t approve! Trump’s “rapid response” team dubbed Mehdi a terrorist-loving “moron,” simply because he called out the White House press secretary’s bald-faced lies. The White House previously branded Zeteo “

They know what we are. And they’re going to keep knowing.

I’ve been a political reporter at multiple mainstream media outlets, including the Daily Beast and Rolling Stone, and I can honestly say that I’ve never been prouder of the work I’ve done than when it’s here with the hard-charging team at Zeteo. You can tell a lot about someone from their enemies. Clearly, Zeteo is getting under Team Trump’s skin; we wouldn’t be doing our jobs right if we weren’t.

But, as always, the only way we can fight back is together.

So if you’re able to, please consider becoming a paid subscriber to

Every paid subscription, every donation, every financial contribution, big or small, sends a message to Donald Trump’s authoritarian, warmongering administration and his party – that we won’t allow ourselves to be bullied, we won’t be silenced, and we won’t stop telling the truth about fascism, genocide, illegal wars, the Republican Party’s widespread efforts to attack free and fair elections, and other moral and literal atrocities of our time.

Please help us fight on. Thank you for standing with Zeteo.

item.title: Russiagate Was Real, Trump Admits
item.description: ⚽️ The World Cup may be coming to an end, but you can support your favorite independent journalism team – Team Zeteo! – year-round with our brand-new football/soccer jerseys! Visit our online store now to purchase them and all of our other Zeteo-branded merch. Paid subscribers get 20% off their…
item.author: "Zeteo | U.S. Politics"
item.content:encoded:
⚽️ The World Cup may be coming to an end, but you can support your favorite independent journalism team – Team Zeteo! – year-round with our brand-new football/soccer jerseys! Visit our online store now to purchase them and all of our other Zeteo-branded merch. Paid subscribers get 20% off their orders!

“Russiagate” was real, according to none other than Donald Trump and his administration.

Trump spent years dismissing allegations that Russia interfered in the 2016 U.S. election to aid his presidential campaign, labeling it a “hoax.” And yet, now his administration has, seemingly unwittingly, admitted that Russia sought to meddle in the 2020 election to boost his failed reelection bid.

“President [Vladimir] Putin and senior Russian officials are overseeing efforts by proxies… to spread claims about former Vice President [Joe] Biden as well as Ukrainian politicians and alleged Ukrainian influence in the 2016 election,” reads a declassified National Intelligence Council document published by Trump’s administration on Thursday.

The White House released documents focused on “Election Integrity,” as Trump delivered a primetime national address alleging foreign election interference. And in the trove were documents that countered the president’s own years-long claims questioning intelligence reports indicating that Russia attempted to interfere in U.S. politics specifically on his behalf.

The Trump administration made much ado about the “revelations” people would learn about on Thursday. Members of the government leaked bits and pieces about the groundbreaking claim the president was set to peddle: that China interfered in U.S. elections.

Trump’s allies whipped up the message publicly, too.

“I was just briefed by the White House on what to expect this evening,” Ohio Senator Bernie Moreno tweeted ominously on Thursday. “I would encourage every American to tune in tonight to the President’s speech. This may be the most important Oval Office address since the Cuban Missile Crisis.”

What actually unfolded was Trump conspiratorially rehashing vulnerabilities that had been known for years in the U.S. intelligence community – and misrepresenting the facts to suggest that the 2020 election outcome, which he lost, was in question. Trump focused primarily on actions by China, rather than other actors like Russia (or Israel, for that matter).

But the documents that the Trump administration itself released show that Joe Biden, and not Donald Trump, was victim of targeted foreign interference during the 2020 election cycle – and Russia was, per the government’s own findings, the main culprit, much more so than China. Indeed, the National Intelligence Council report states outright that “Beijing did not intend to try to affect the election.”

Not so for Russia. The report says that Russia was using “state media, social media, and other online media platforms to denigrate the former Vice President, the Democratic Party, and the U.S. political ‘establishment’ and to promote divisions in the country.”

Per the report, Russia pushed claims about how Joe Biden, as vice president, “engaged in criminal activity in his dealings with Ukraine and individuals tied to Ukrainian energy firm Burisma.” The document states that actors affiliated with the Russian government were “advancing such narratives with US officials and other prominent persons.”

It continues: “These figures are conspiring to intensify their efforts as the election approaches to orchestrate a high-profile corruption scandal implicating former Vice President Biden and the Democratic Party at the peak of the 2020 US presidential campaign. Their aim is to defeat the former Vice President and ensure the President’s victory.”

One has to wonder if Trump even read the documents he thought were so important to reveal to the public.

The document also adds something that may sound familiar: “Russian actors have circulated narratives about voter fraud resulting from mail-in balloting and called some U.S. primary voting processes ‘rigged’ by the Democratic Party.”

It’s a claim that Trump himself has made an uncountable number of times, including Thursday night, when he said that “if you watched California and so many other places, mail-in ballots are inherently corrupt.”

Meanwhile, Trump has repeatedly claimed that the notion of Russia interfering in U.S. elections was a hoax.

In May 2017, after former FBI Director Robert Mueller was appointed as special counsel to oversee the investigation into alleged Russian interference in the 2016 election and potential ties to the Trump campaign, Trump tweeted, “This is the single greatest witch hunt of a politician in American history!”

In April 2019, after Mueller’s report was released – which concluded that Russia interfered in the 2016 election, but did not establish that Russia conspired with the Trump campaign to do so – Trump reiterated that the investigation was “an Illegally Started Hoax that never should have happened.”

Trump repeated the claim throughout the 2024 election, and even afterwards.

“Obama himself manufactured the Russia, Russia, Russia HOAX,” he posted on his social media app, Truth Social, last year. “Crooked Hillary, Sleepy Joe, and numerous others participated in this, THE CRIME OF THE CENTURY!. Irrefutable EVIDENCE. A major threat to our Country!!!”

A few months later, after sharing a phone call with Russian President Vladimir Putin, Trump told reporters: “Don’t forget, we went through the “Russia, Russia, Russia” hoax together.

And he’d call me, I’d call him. I’d say, ‘Can you believe the stuff that they’re making up?’ And it turned out we were right. They made it all up.”

Of course, it wasn’t just Robert Mueller suggesting Russia had attempted to interfere in U.S. elections.

A 2017 Intelligence Community Assessment concluded that Russia meddled in the 2016 U.S. election, and specifically to aid Trump.

A multi-volume report released by the Republican-led Senate Intelligence Committee between 2019 and 2020 found that the Russian government had pursued an aggressive campaign to interfere in the election on Trump’s behalf, and that there were numerous points of contact between Trump officials and Russian operatives.

The acting head of the committee was none other than Marco Rubio, who at the time said they found “irrefutable evidence of Russian meddling.”

Six years later, as Rubio serves at Trump’s beck-and-call, Trump’s own administration has now released documents attesting that Russia sought to aid his 2020 campaign, too.

item.title: This Week in Palestine – Israel Attacks Funeral as It Escalates Strikes on Gaza
item.description: Palestinians must live their lives on repeat. Israeli strikes – day and night, targeting hospitals, police, and children. Kids lose one parent and then another. Parents cry over the body of a son, and then a daughter. Homes are demolished . Israeli settlers attack Palestinians on their own land –…
item.author: This Week in Palestine | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Palestinians must live their lives on repeat. Israeli strikes – day and night, targeting hospitals, police, and children. Kids lose one parent and then another. Parents cry over the body of a son, and then a daughter. Homes are demolished. Israeli settlers attack Palestinians on their own land – over and over again.

It’s a never-ending cycle – one caught on video, shared online, yet allowed to continue, a cycle that only gets worse.

A U.S. politician experienced only a small sliver of the kind of violence Palestinians endure every day, only to find himself on the receiving end of the relentless hasbara industry this week. At the same time, more children were killed, a Palestinian family had 30 minutes to collect as many of their belongings as possible before their home was demolished, and Israel targeted a funeral in Gaza.

Here’s more on all of that and much more, as it was another troubling week in Palestine:

One year after Israeli settlers beat to death Palestinian-American Sayfollah Musallet as he visited his family in the occupied West Bank, no one has been arrested for his murder, his family said.

Instead, journalists, including a CNN crew, covering the one-year anniversary at the spot Musallet was killed were attacked by settlers. Israeli police said four Israelis were arrested, but such arrests often do not result in lengthy or meaningful punishment.

On Saturday, U.S. Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna said he was detained by Israeli settlers and soldiers during his Palestinian-led trip to the occupied West Bank. The trip and his comments were immediately attacked by Israel and its supporters, who attempted to say Khanna never coordinated his visit with the Israeli military, despite the fact he was on Palestinian land.

Watch Mehdi’s interview with Khanna after the trip:

Palestinian authorities said Israeli gunfire killed 9-year-old Tala Abu Matar in Al-Bureij refugee camp in central Gaza.

In a gutting video shared on social media, believed to be of Tala’s mother, she cries, “Get up, my baby, come back to me.”

Israel killed at least five others across Gaza on Sunday, despite the so-called “ceasefire.”

Israeli forces killed a Palestinian man near the separation wall in the town of Bir Nabala, northeast of Jerusalem. AFP, citing Palestinian officials, said the man was trying to get to “his workplace inside the city to support his family.”

Israel, which severely restricts Palestinians’ movement to Jerusalem, has killed more than 35 Palestinians near the wall this year, Drop Site reported.

Israeli forces demolished a Palestinian home in the occupied West Bank village of Ad-Deriat, near Hebron, despite a lawyer saying it had protection under a court order, Al Jazeera reported.

The house, home to two families, including eight children, was built in 2016 with the proper permits, the families said. Israeli forces reportedly gave the families only 30 minutes to collect their belongings before beginning the demolition.

In a historic vote, 103 Democrats, including lawmakers like former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, joined Republican Rep. Thomas Massie to support cutting military aid to Israel.

As Zeteo’s Prem Thakker noted in ‘First Draft’ after the vote, “Not long ago, that would have been a completely unthinkable outcome,” and underscores “just how toxic pro-Israel politics is becoming.”

Physicians for Human Rights Israel (PHRI) issued another dire warning about the condition of Gaza Dr. Hussam Abu Safiya, who has been held captive by Israel without charge since December 2024.

Meanwhile, Israeli ultranationalist National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir celebrated a decision by the environment minister to reclassify the Nile crocodile as a "tended wild animal" to allow Ben-Gvir to create his “crocodile prison” for Palestinians, Israeli media reported. The decision was made over objections from the Environment Ministry's legal adviser and environmental groups.

Ben-Gvir, who has been touting the idea since last December, was reportedly inspired by Florida’s now-shuttered “Alligator Alcatraz.”

Israel killed at least eight Palestinians and wounded more than a dozen others in a strike on a gathering for a funeral of another Palestinian killed earlier in the Nuseirat refugee camp in central Gaza. Several other Palestinians were killed in other Israeli attacks across the Strip.

Israeli strikes have surged to their highest level since the so-called ceasefire took effect last October, a conflict monitor reported this week.

mosab_abutoha

1,070 likes

Miss last week’s edition? Catch up here. Plus, check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: The Real Winner of the World Cup? FIFA
item.description: As the World Cup draws to a close, all eyes are on Spain and Argentina . But whether we like it or not, our eyes will also be diverted to Donald Trump . For the first time all tournament, Trump will be in attendance for a match. And as we know from last year’s Club World Cup and U.S. Open , as well…
item.author: "Zeteo | Dark Side of the World Cup "
item.content:encoded:
As the World Cup draws to a close, all eyes are on Spain and Argentina. But whether we like it or not, our eyes will also be diverted to Donald Trump. For the first time all tournament, Trump will be in attendance for a match. And as we know from last year’s Club World Cup and U.S. Open, as well as last month’s NBA Finals, when Trump shows up to a sports event, the story inevitably becomes about him.

Trump’s far-right pal, Argentine President Javier Milei was expected to be in attendance on Sunday too, and together they would have presented the trophy to Lionel Messi and his teammates in the case of an Argentina win. Thankfully, we’ve been spared the possibility of a Milei-Trump joint appearance because of Milei’s superstitions.

Still, a win will provide a boost to Milei, and for Trump, the final will mark the culmination of an event that’s served as a distraction from his various crimes and misdeeds. FIFA allowing authoritarian leaders to use the World Cup to their benefit is an age-old story – one we’ve documented throughout this three-part series, dating back to Italy under Benito Mussolini during the 1934 tournament.

As the New Yorker’s Ishaan Tharoor puts it, that tournament was “a wonderful showcase for Italy…a showcase for the preeminence and prowess of its fascist regime.”

Join Mehdi, Ishaan, Political Scientist Jules Boykoff, and FIFA whistleblower Bonita Mersiades for a timely unpacking of the dark side of this incredibly cherished international tournament, and FIFA’s role in it.

Paid subscribers can watch the full episode. Free subscribers can watch a 1-minute preview. Become a paid subscriber to Zeteo today to support the work we’re doing and to get access to all our premium content!

item.title: Trump Has Given Us a ‘Golden Age’ – Of Corruption and Cruelty
item.description: They cannot competently govern, but give Donald Trump’s flunkies this much: They know what their president wants to hear. “You have changed America and created the Golden Age,” Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick has gushed . “A new Golden Age of economic prosperity,” boasted Treasury Secretary Scott…
item.author: "Zeteo | U.S. Politics"
item.content:encoded:
They cannot competently govern, but give Donald Trump’s flunkies this much: They know what their president wants to hear.

“You have changed America and created the Golden Age,” Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick has gushed. “A new Golden Age of economic prosperity,” boasted Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. “A golden age of national defense,” chirped the Pentagon’s Pete Hegseth.

And “the Golden Age,” Interior Secretary Doug Burgum told Americans on the country’s 250th birthday, “is just beginning.”

OK, laugh at their fantasies. Trump’s bungling has driven inflation higher, his Iran debacle drags on, his ICE agents keep killing peaceable, productive immigrants, and his vanity projects produce one embarrassment after another. Leaders presiding over halcyon days do not suffer sub-40% public approval.

But a narcissistic president who craves superlatives deserves recognition for those he has earned. He has indeed created a Golden Age – of the most appalling kinds.

Over and over, in myriad ways, his second presidency elevates the worst of America. Take just the last few weeks.

Trump’s Golden Age of Ignorance, disdaining history, medical science, and technological reality, has produced swift results.

In late June, soon after Hegseth eliminated mandatory flu vaccines for service members, Lackland Air Force Base reported more than 200 flu infections; one trainee died. On Friday, Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s vaccine-skeptical Health and Human Services Department reported that 2026 measles cases have nearly reached the total for all of 2025 – America’s highest in a quarter-century.

Earlier this month, Energy Secretary Chris Wright boasted that the administration would end government subsidies for new wind and solar energy projects. “The wind doesn’t always blow and the sun doesn’t always shine,” Wright explained, sounding sensible to anyone unaware that 21st century batteries store clean, renewable energy for later use. (In May, solar power passed dirty, costlier coal as a source of U.S. electricity.)

Trump’s Golden Age of Delusion requires millions of Americans – perhaps including the president himself – to believe things that are not true. When a group of white supremacists on July 4 embarrassed the right with a public parade, prominent MAGA voices suggested they were really left-wingers in disguise.

It echoed old claims that Jan. 6 insurrectionists who violently tried to overturn the 2020 election had been members of antifa – a fever dream that broke when Trump pardoned them.

In a Thursday night speech, Trump regurgitated his nonsensical claims about the 2020 election to justify interfering in midterm contests this fall. Major television networks declined to surrender their airtime for his rant.

On the level of farce, Trump covered the administration’s incompetence by claiming vandals destroyed its putative upgrade of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. His hapless U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia, Jeannine Pirro, claimed in a felony indictment that a former U.S. Olympic athlete “forcefully and violently” ripped up pool sealant by hand.

Trump’s Golden Age of Retreat keeps shrinking America’s influence abroad and moral standing at home.

At last week’s NATO summit in Turkey, the democratic allies Trump has alienated used flattery to keep the erratic president, who prefers autocratic rulers, from throwing a tantrum. As if shielding the fragile ego of a child, Belgium’s Prime Minister pledged not to bring up his country’s World Cup soccer win over the U.S. Less restrained, victorious Belgium team members mocked Trump for his crude intervention with international soccer officials to restore the eligibility of a previously suspended American player.

Shortly before Independence Day, the Trump-molded Supreme Court shrugged off his flagrantly racist rhetoric to declare the administration’s termination of Temporary Protected Status for Haitians constitutionally permissible; earlier, the court gutted the 1965 Voting Rights Act. On July 6, a White House report condemned the Smithsonian Institution staff, run by the African American scholar Lonnie Bunch, as “radical activists” with “anti-white” views.

Trump’s Golden Age of Cruelty relentlessly shocks the American conscience. This week in Maine, ICE gunned down a young Colombian immigrant, letting him bleed out in front of his wife and small child. Joan Sebastián Guerrero, working legally with a U.S. Social Security number, was not even the man ICE had been looking for.

The same thing happened last week in Texas to Lorenzo Salgado Araujo. The 52-year-old Mexican national, with no criminal record, had worked construction here for 35 years and sent three sons to college.

Recently, the Dallas County medical examiner ruled the death of Afghan national Mohammad Nazeer Paktiawal an accident. Paktiawal, who served alongside U.S. Special Forces at war against the Taliban, died a day after ICE agents detained him. His wife says he was denied his asthma inhaler.

Trump cares most about his Golden Era of Sleaze. The New York Times last month calculated his 2025 revenue at $2.2 billion. He has profited from opening a bazaar for presidential pardons, including one for a crypto titan linked to his family business, and from cryptocurrency legislation the Republican-led Congress sent him to sign.

As Trump boosts defense spending, his children have affiliated companies winning fat Pentagon contracts. Embarrassment caused by the $400 million airplane the president took from the Qatari government – which the Secret Service made him leave behind in Turkey due to security vulnerabilities – hasn’t slowed the cash-grab.

Decorative baubles Trump has slapped on the walls make the White House look like a cross between Middle East tyrant’s palace and Las Vegas brothel. If only the Golden Age of Cheap Gold were the worst of it.

John Harwood is the former chief Washington correspondent for CNBC and White House correspondent for CNN. He has interviewed every president from George H.W. Bush to Joe Biden. Sign up for ‘The Stakes with John Harwood’ to get all of his columns in your inbox.

item.title: Stephen Miller Expands His Apocalyptic War on the Left
item.description: On this day in 1984, at the Democratic National Convention in San Francisco, Rev. Jesse Jackson delivered his ‘Rainbow Coalition’ speech, calling for government in the name of the many, not the few. Alas, the eventual nominee, Walter Mondale, lost 49 of 50 states to President Ronald Reagan. Happy…
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1984, at the Democratic National Convention in San Francisco, Rev. Jesse Jackson delivered his ‘Rainbow Coalition’ speech, calling for government in the name of the many, not the few. Alas, the eventual nominee, Walter Mondale, lost 49 of 50 states to President Ronald Reagan.

Happy Friday! It’s Andrew here, looking forward to the weekend – or as Donald Trump understands it, the days he prefers to make news and war and ramp up his authoritarian blitz. Perhaps he’ll go easy on us, because yesterday was truly enough: If you thought Trump’s lying address in primetime was bad, wait until you see what fresh hell Stephen Miller has in mind for all of us.

In today’s ‘First Draft,’ we unpack Miller’s speech about “left-wing violence,” take in Trump’s mess of election fraud lies, consider the brewing disaster over Iran and the Strait of Hormuz, and take a look at what happened when acting Attorney General Todd Blanche was finally forced to meet a group of Epstein victims. Let’s read on.

Stephen Miller is the most powerful unelected figure in Washington. His title is deputy White House deputy chief of staff, but the true nature of his role lands somewhere between Trump’s policy architect, and the actual U.S. attorney general, and the real Homeland Security chief – or perhaps the shadow president.

As I’ve reported with my colleague Swin, “Everything you loathe or love about Donald Trump’s America, you hate or cherish about Stephen Miller’s republic of fear.”

Miller has been given enormous latitude to carry out the president’s agenda, and his own, despite helping tank Trump’s poll numbers on his signature issue, immigration, by leading a racist mass-deportation campaign too cruel, too violent, and too in-your-face for Americans with a conscience to stomach. But that is why Trump keeps Miller around and empowered: Trump and Miller are both fascists, and Miller is Trump’s fascism strategist. He is the guy who gets it done.

A Rush Limbaugh fan as a teen, Miller has always dreamed of crushing the left. Now, he’s getting to use the full force of the federal government to lead an all-out war on liberals, progressives, and leftists – and he’s throwing it in all our faces with his characteristically apocalyptic and authoritarian rhetoric.

“We have taken the necessary and essential action of formally recognizing left-wing violence as a form of political terrorism that is a direct threat to our national security and the survival of our republican form of government,” Miller said Thursday.

Speaking at the State Department, as part of what the administration billed a “Ministerial on the Resurgence of Political Terrorism,” Miller touted Trump’s NSPM-7, a national security presidential memorandum that directs intelligence and law enforcement agencies “to work together to disrupt, identify, defund, debank, arrest, and prosecute these political terrorists that are operating in our country,” as he put it.

Complaining nebulously of juries refusing to convict people “obviously guilty of a crime,” such as left-wing individuals “who’ve committed assaults against ICE officers,” Miller decided this “demonstrates how deeply the cancer has begun to infest your society.” He did not give specifics, though the Trump administration has had to drop a series of cases against anti-ICE protesters and people accused of assaulting ICE officers, after evidence suggested the government was lying. Or perhaps he meant the “sandwich guy.”

Miller claimed ICE agents – the violent, masked thugs who kidnap people, terrorize communities, and kill immigrants and U.S. citizens alike – are the real victims, “subjected to an over 8,000% increase in violent assaults.”

“This is repeat, systemic, organized, funded insurrection and armed resistance against the federal government,” he said.

There is, of course, not always video evidence to verify what ICE agents claim – especially since they don’t appear to be using the body cameras Congress wants them to. But when there has been video evidence, we’ve seen ICE and the Trump administration, including Stephen Miller, caught in obvious, heinous lies. For instance: when Miller called Alex Pretti, a nurse murdered by federal agents occupying Minneapolis, a “would-be assassin” who “tried to murder federal law enforcement.”

There’s surely more to fact-check in Miller’s speech, such as his claim that no one at “violent antifa demonstrations,” wherever those are, ever looks normal: “Every one of them, through the course of their life and their decisions, has scarred their body and their appearance in many different ways to the point in which their outer appearance becomes a manifestation of their inner hatred.” Fact check. Fact check! Fact check!!!

But the outright lying, performed with an unusually off-putting Southern California accent you can’t unhear, is not even close to the worst thing about Miller.

What’s worst is he’s leading Trump’s fascism strategy as it picks up a full head of steam.

It means Marco Rubio’s State Department announcing a “visa restriction policy” on Thursday “that targets members of Far-Left Terrorist and other aligned groups who have supported or incited acts of terrorism” or “participated in economic sabotage.” If I were a betting man, I might wager that “economic sabotage” means: boycotting Israel.

It means counterterrorism czar Sebastian Gorka publishing a U.S. counterterrorism strategy asserting the government currently faces three main types of terror groups: “Narcoterrorists and Transnational Gangs,” “Legacy Islamist Terrorists,” and “Violent Left-Wing Extremists, including Anarchists and Anti-Fascists.”

It means Gorka claiming that “right now, we are not seeing comparable trend lines to violence on the right as we see on the left.” That is, of course, not true. A 2024 study by the Justice Department’s research arm found that far-right attacks “outpace all other types of terrorism and domestic violent extremism,” and that, since 1990, “far-right extremists have committed far more ideologically motivated homicides than far-left or radical Islamist extremists.”

Trump’s DOJ deleted the study from its website. But the Anti-Defamation League – no friend of the left – similarly reported several years ago: “Of the 444 people killed by extremists from 2013 to 2022, 335 of them, 75% of them, were killed by right-wing extremists.”

The National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START), at the University of Maryland, studied acts of extremism in the U.S. between 1948 and 2018, and concluded that “right-wing actors are significantly more violent than left-wing actors.”

The facts, however, are not the point. An administration concerned about political violence would not see its president offer mass pardons and clemency to the rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6 as part of an effort to overturn the 2020 election, nor would it demand judges throw out related seditious conspiracy convictions against Proud Boys and Oath Keepers.

No, this administration is led by fascists, like Stephen Miller, who is living out his violent fantasy.

  • More war:The U.S.launchedanother wave of strikes, hitting areas around the Strait of Hormuz as it continued to enforce a naval blockade and also striking further north, around energy infrastructure. Iran launched missiles toward the U.S. targets across the region.
  • Bridges hit:An Iranian military spokespersonsaidTehran could attack “all the infrastructure in the region,” if the U.S. followed through on Trump’s repeated threat to target bridges and power plants, which would be a war crime. Later, reportssaidthe U.S. had hit highway and railway bridges, atelecoms installation, and akey towerthat oversees commercial traffic at Iran’s Chabahar port.
  • Strait talk:Iranian spokesperson Col. Ebrahim Zolfaghariadded: “Under no circumstances and in no way will we allow America, as a foreign and extraregional country, to interfere in the Strait of Hormuz. This is Iran’s invincible red line.”
  • Fuel on the fire:Iran told Houthi rebels in Yemen to be ready to close the Red Sea oil route if the U.S. hits Iranian power facilities, Reuterssaid. At the pumps in the U.S., gas pricesclimbedtowards $4 a gallon while diesel topped $5. Traderstold the Financial Timesoil reserves were close to running out.
  • Budget fight:U.S. House Republicans remainedmiredin intra-party discontent over Trump’s $95 billion budget ask, needed in large part to keep the war going.

Donald Trump spoke to the nation last night, and it was predictably mad, as he made wild claims about supposed massive Chinese interference in U.S. elections, pitched for his voter-suppression SAVE Act, and rehearsed other familiar talking points, half-truths, and outright lies. Fortunately for Zeteo readers, Mehdi, Swin and special guest Rep. Jamie Raskin were on hand to break down what Mehdi called Trump’s “series of outlandish, unsubstantiated, conspiratorial, and self-serving claims about the 2020 election,” and what Raskin, a former House impeachment manager, saw as “gibberish and drivel.”

  • Police state:The ICE agent who killed a man in Maine this week has a history of violent behavior and mental health struggles, APreported, citing family and court records. The Interceptsaidagents in the shooting wore body cameras not switched on. The ACLUreported214 children “were detained, targeted for enforcement, or experienced law enforcement misconduct” by immigration agents. The White Housemovedto deny green cards to immigrants using public benefits.
  • Insider trading:CNNsaidTrump has promoted more than 20 companies after buying stock, sometimes announcing actions likely to boost stock value. The White House denied wrongdoing. An analyst said, “This is an ethics disaster.” Truth Social said it would sell Wall Street firms quicker access to market-moving Trump posts.Really.
  • Insider reading:Trump’s teleprompter operator made more than $100,000 betting on the contents of Trump’s speeches, ABC Newsreported. The aide, Gabe Perez, was put on unpaid leave amid an investigation, the White House said.
  • Scraping bottom:TheWashington PostshowedTrump’s Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool renovation was not vandalized as he claims, just poorly done.
  • Special relationship:As Democrats seek looser ties with Israel, Pennsylvania Senator John Fettermantold NBChe would leave the party if such stances reached its platform, saying: “That’s a red line for me.” After Rep. Pat Ryan voted to block aid to Israel and said he doesn’t want AIPAC’s support, the pro-Israel lobbyaccusedhim of launching “an attack on American citizens, vilifying their beliefs and participation in our democracy.”
  • Cash lag:Democratic National Committee members were asked to sign NDAs before a meeting about finances, Axiosreported, in another blow for chair Ken Martin. Heading into the midterms, the DNC is way behind the RNC on funds raised.
  • Taco hell:Investigators identified a potential source of the outbreak of cyclosporiasis, the condition that causes explosive diarrhea, in a company supplying lettuce to Taco Bell, theWashington Postreported.

On day two of hearings concerning the nomination of Todd “I love working for President Trump” Blanche to be attorney general of the United States, Republican Senator Thom Tillis of North Carolina made a surprise move. He would not support Blanche, he said – thereby providing the one GOP defection needed to sink the nomination – unless Blanche met with survivors of abuse by Jeffrey Epstein, the late financier, sex offender and close Trump friend.

The meeting happened. Attendees’ reactions were not positive. Eric Michael Garcia of The Independent reported a text message from a survivor, Lara Blume McGee:

“Today, Epstein survivors confronted Todd Blanche – and he treated it like a perfunctory audition for votes, not accountability. Blanche was condescending, repeatedly interrupted and tried to gaslight survivors instead of explaining why DOJ released materials that revealed survivors’ identities. He offered no real plan to investigate anyone beyond Epstein and [Ghislaine] Maxwell and gave no reason to believe the remaining files aren’t vital to exposing other perpetrators and enablers.

“Todd Blanche is unfit to be attorney general – Senator Thom Tillis and the Senate must vote no.”

Tillis, however, seemed satisfied, posting: “I commend Todd Blanche for doing what all his predecessors over the last two decades never did: meet with the victims of Jeffrey Epstein’s horrific crimes. I appreciate his willingness to directly engage and listen to them.”

They don’t call Washington the Swamp for nothing. Even after a whole day in the aircon, you feel in need of a shower.

What ancient rite did John Ashcroft, on Thursday a character witness for Todd Blanche, insist on when becoming governor of Missouri, a U.S. senator, and ultimately attorney general under George W. Bush?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

  • 🇵🇸 Don’t forget Gaza:Israeli strikes killed at least five Palestinians, officialssaid, as attacks surged to levels not seen since the ceasefire in name only took effect last October. Meanwhile, Trump’s so-called “Board of Peace” has shrunk plans for rebuilding Gaza to a small pilot project in the south,TheGuardianreported, adding that no substantial progress is expected before Israel’s elections on Oct. 27.

  • 🇺🇦

    Reshuffle kerfuffle:Thousandsprotestedafter President Volodymyr Zelensky replaced his defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, citing friction with Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi, commander of Ukraine’s armed forces. “I’m just showing that if the sides can’t resolve an issue, I will have to resolve it,” Zelenskyy said.

  • 🇬🇧🇦🇷

    Cup of woe:The UKurgedFIFA to investigate after Argentina players displayed a banner claiming sovereignty over the Falkland Islands, or Las Malvinas, after beating England in the World Cup semi-final. “Self-determination rests with the islanders and our commitment to the Falklands will never waver,” Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s spokesperson said.

  • 🇨🇩🇺🇬

    Ebola update:Patients and health workers fled when a hospital in DR Congo wasattackedby a crowd angered by the death of a pregnant woman. Several Ebola patients escaped, officials said. Ugandadischargedits last recorded Ebola patient, triggering a 42-day countdown to be free of the virus.


Missouri Senator Eric Schmitt wants Donald Trump to annex Greenland because… Muslims. Okay.

The Woman in the Photo Wanted to Be Alone. Instead, She Went Viral

Ellie Silverman of NOTUS tells the story of Bernita Bowlding, the Black woman seen in a viral picture of masked white supremacists riding the DC Metro. As the picture spread, Bowlding disappeared. When she came back, Silverman writes, she “didn’t know that the photo had gone around the world. She didn’t know she was strong and brave and a symbol of our division and a vessel for our fears. She didn’t know that people who didn’t know her thought they knew her. The icon of America wanted to go now. She needed to pace the parking lot, to sit on the curb, to have some quiet.”

Trump’s Ex-Campaign Manager Is Running An Israeli Influence Operation Targeting the MAGA Base

Brad Parscale, the former Trump campaign manager previously seen shirtless and rugby tackled by Florida police, is the subject of a fascinating piece by Eric Cortalessa of Time, about work with online influencers on behalf of Israel, fueling discord on the U.S. right. “Publicly, the campaign was framed as an effort to combat rising antisemitism online,” Cortalessa writes. “An Israeli Foreign Ministry official familiar with the arrangement says there was another strategic aim: preventing young conservatives from turning against Israel.”

How a Brooklyn coffee shop got caught in a political firestorm

For USA Today, Amethyst Martinez considers how New York chain Poetica Coffee got into an online fight with Rep. Dan Goldman, the pro-Israel Democrat who lost his U.S. House seat to a left-wing challenger last month. Safe to say, no one, from the chain where “the guest is sacred, the books are unbanned, and the door is open to everyone,” to the congressman, to the Trump apparatchiks that leaped to capitalize, comes out unscathed. This is what happens when social media runs our lives.

Zeteo’s Martin Pengelly contributed to this newsletter.

item.title: Stephen Miller Expands His Apocalyptic War on the Left
item.description: On this day in 1984, at the Democratic National Convention in San Francisco, Rev. Jesse Jackson delivered his ‘Rainbow Coalition’ speech, calling for government in the name of the many, not the few. Alas, the eventual nominee, Walter Mondale, lost 49 of 50 states to President Ronald Reagan. Happy…
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1984, at the Democratic National Convention in San Francisco, Rev. Jesse Jackson delivered his ‘Rainbow Coalition’ speech, calling for government in the name of the many, not the few. Alas, the eventual nominee, Walter Mondale, lost 49 of 50 states to President Ronald Reagan.

Happy Friday! It’s Andrew here, looking forward to the weekend – or as Donald Trump understands it, the days he prefers to make news and war and ramp up his authoritarian blitz. Perhaps he’ll go easy on us, because yesterday was truly enough: If you thought Trump’s lying address in primetime was bad, wait until you see what fresh hell Stephen Miller has in mind for all of us.

In today’s ‘First Draft,’ we unpack Miller’s speech about “left-wing violence,” take in Trump’s mess of election fraud lies, consider the brewing disaster over Iran and the Strait of Hormuz, and take a look at what happened when acting Attorney General Todd Blanche was finally forced to meet a group of Epstein victims. Let’s read on.

Stephen Miller is the most powerful unelected figure in Washington. His title is deputy White House deputy chief of staff, but the true nature of his role lands somewhere between Trump’s policy architect, and the actual U.S. attorney general, and the real Homeland Security chief – or perhaps the shadow president.

As I’ve reported with my colleague Swin, “Everything you loathe or love about Donald Trump’s America, you hate or cherish about Stephen Miller’s republic of fear.”

Miller has been given enormous latitude to carry out the president’s agenda, and his own, despite helping tank Trump’s poll numbers on his signature issue, immigration, by leading a racist mass-deportation campaign too cruel, too violent, and too in-your-face for Americans with a conscience to stomach. But that is why Trump keeps Miller around and empowered: Trump and Miller are both fascists, and Miller is Trump’s fascism strategist. He is the guy who gets it done.

A Rush Limbaugh fan as a teen, Miller has always dreamed of crushing the left. Now, he’s getting to use the full force of the federal government to lead an all-out war on liberals, progressives, and leftists – and he’s throwing it in all our faces with his characteristically apocalyptic and authoritarian rhetoric.

“We have taken the necessary and essential action of formally recognizing left-wing violence as a form of political terrorism that is a direct threat to our national security and the survival of our republican form of government,” Miller said Thursday.

Speaking at the State Department, as part of what the administration billed a “Ministerial on the Resurgence of Political Terrorism,” Miller touted Trump’s NSPM-7, a national security presidential memorandum that directs intelligence and law enforcement agencies “to work together to disrupt, identify, defund, debank, arrest, and prosecute these political terrorists that are operating in our country,” as he put it.

Complaining nebulously of juries refusing to convict people “obviously guilty of a crime,” such as left-wing individuals “who’ve committed assaults against ICE officers,” Miller decided this “demonstrates how deeply the cancer has begun to infest your society.” He did not give specifics, though the Trump administration has had to drop a series of cases against anti-ICE protesters and people accused of assaulting ICE officers, after evidence suggested the government was lying. Or perhaps he meant the “sandwich guy.”

Miller claimed ICE agents – the violent, masked thugs who kidnap people, terrorize communities, and kill immigrants and U.S. citizens alike – are the real victims, “subjected to an over 8,000% increase in violent assaults.”

“This is repeat, systemic, organized, funded insurrection and armed resistance against the federal government,” he said.

There is, of course, not always video evidence to verify what ICE agents claim – especially since they don’t appear to be using the body cameras Congress wants them to. But when there has been video evidence, we’ve seen ICE and the Trump administration, including Stephen Miller, caught in obvious, heinous lies. For instance: when Miller called Alex Pretti, a nurse murdered by federal agents occupying Minneapolis, a “would-be assassin” who “tried to murder federal law enforcement.”

There’s surely more to fact-check in Miller’s speech, such as his claim that no one at “violent antifa demonstrations,” wherever those are, ever looks normal: “Every one of them, through the course of their life and their decisions, has scarred their body and their appearance in many different ways to the point in which their outer appearance becomes a manifestation of their inner hatred.” Fact check. Fact check! Fact check!!!

But the outright lying, performed with an unusually off-putting Southern California accent you can’t unhear, is not even close to the worst thing about Miller.

What’s worst is he’s leading Trump’s fascism strategy as it picks up a full head of steam.

It means Marco Rubio’s State Department announcing a “visa restriction policy” on Thursday “that targets members of Far-Left Terrorist and other aligned groups who have supported or incited acts of terrorism” or “participated in economic sabotage.” If I were a betting man, I might wager that “economic sabotage” means: boycotting Israel.

It means counterterrorism czar Sebastian Gorka publishing a U.S. counterterrorism strategy asserting the government currently faces three main types of terror groups: “Narcoterrorists and Transnational Gangs,” “Legacy Islamist Terrorists,” and “Violent Left-Wing Extremists, including Anarchists and Anti-Fascists.”

It means Gorka claiming that “right now, we are not seeing comparable trend lines to violence on the right as we see on the left.” That is, of course, not true. A 2024 study by the Justice Department’s research arm found that far-right attacks “outpace all other types of terrorism and domestic violent extremism,” and that, since 1990, “far-right extremists have committed far more ideologically motivated homicides than far-left or radical Islamist extremists.”

Trump’s DOJ deleted the study from its website. But the Anti-Defamation League – no friend of the left – similarly reported several years ago: “Of the 444 people killed by extremists from 2013 to 2022, 335 of them, 75% of them, were killed by right-wing extremists.”

The National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START), at the University of Maryland, studied acts of extremism in the U.S. between 1948 and 2018, and concluded that “right-wing actors are significantly more violent than left-wing actors.”

The facts, however, are not the point. An administration concerned about political violence would not see its president offer mass pardons and clemency to the rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6 as part of an effort to overturn the 2020 election, nor would it demand judges throw out related seditious conspiracy convictions against Proud Boys and Oath Keepers.

No, this administration is led by fascists, like Stephen Miller, who is living out his violent fantasy.

  • More war:The U.S.launchedanother wave of strikes, hitting areas around the Strait of Hormuz as it continued to enforce a naval blockade and also striking further north, around energy infrastructure. Iran launched missiles toward the U.S. targets across the region.
  • Bridges hit:An Iranian military spokespersonsaidTehran could attack “all the infrastructure in the region,” if the U.S. followed through on Trump’s repeated threat to target bridges and power plants, which would be a war crime. Later, reportssaidthe U.S. had hit highway and railway bridges, atelecoms installation, and akey towerthat oversees commercial traffic at Iran’s Chabahar port.
  • Strait talk:Iranian spokesperson Col. Ebrahim Zolfaghariadded: “Under no circumstances and in no way will we allow America, as a foreign and extraregional country, to interfere in the Strait of Hormuz. This is Iran’s invincible red line.”
  • Fuel on the fire:Iran told Houthi rebels in Yemen to be ready to close the Red Sea oil route if the U.S. hits Iranian power facilities, Reuterssaid. At the pumps in the U.S., gas pricesclimbedtowards $4 a gallon while diesel topped $5. Traderstold the Financial Timesoil reserves were close to running out.
  • Budget fight:U.S. House Republicans remainedmiredin intra-party discontent over Trump’s $95 billion budget ask, needed in large part to keep the war going.

Donald Trump spoke to the nation last night, and it was predictably mad, as he made wild claims about supposed massive Chinese interference in U.S. elections, pitched for his voter-suppression SAVE Act, and rehearsed other familiar talking points, half-truths, and outright lies. Fortunately for Zeteo readers, Mehdi, Swin and special guest Rep. Jamie Raskin were on hand to break down what Mehdi called Trump’s “series of outlandish, unsubstantiated, conspiratorial, and self-serving claims about the 2020 election,” and what Raskin, a former House impeachment manager, saw as “gibberish and drivel.”

  • Police state:The ICE agent who killed a man in Maine this week has a history of violent behavior and mental health struggles, APreported, citing family and court records. The Interceptsaidagents in the shooting wore body cameras not switched on. The ACLUreported214 children “were detained, targeted for enforcement, or experienced law enforcement misconduct” by immigration agents. The White Housemovedto deny green cards to immigrants using public benefits.
  • Insider trading:CNNsaidTrump has promoted more than 20 companies after buying stock, sometimes announcing actions likely to boost stock value. The White House denied wrongdoing. An analyst said, “This is an ethics disaster.” Truth Social said it would sell Wall Street firms quicker access to market-moving Trump posts.Really.
  • Insider reading:Trump’s teleprompter operator made more than $100,000 betting on the contents of Trump’s speeches, ABC Newsreported. The aide, Gabe Perez, was put on unpaid leave amid an investigation, the White House said.
  • Scraping bottom:TheWashington PostshowedTrump’s Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool renovation was not vandalized as he claims, just poorly done.
  • Special relationship:As Democrats seek looser ties with Israel, Pennsylvania Senator John Fettermantold NBChe would leave the party if such stances reached its platform, saying: “That’s a red line for me.” After Rep. Pat Ryan voted to block aid to Israel and said he doesn’t want AIPAC’s support, the pro-Israel lobbyaccusedhim of launching “an attack on American citizens, vilifying their beliefs and participation in our democracy.”
  • Cash lag:Democratic National Committee members were asked to sign NDAs before a meeting about finances, Axiosreported, in another blow for chair Ken Martin. Heading into the midterms, the DNC is way behind the RNC on funds raised.
  • Taco hell:Investigators identified a potential source of the outbreak of cyclosporiasis, the condition that causes explosive diarrhea, in a company supplying lettuce to Taco Bell, theWashington Postreported.

On day two of hearings concerning the nomination of Todd “I love working for President Trump” Blanche to be attorney general of the United States, Republican Senator Thom Tillis of North Carolina made a surprise move. He would not support Blanche, he said – thereby providing the one GOP defection needed to sink the nomination – unless Blanche met with survivors of abuse by Jeffrey Epstein, the late financier, sex offender and close Trump friend.

The meeting happened. Attendees’ reactions were not positive. Eric Michael Garcia of The Independent reported a text message from a survivor, Lara Blume McGee:

“Today, Epstein survivors confronted Todd Blanche – and he treated it like a perfunctory audition for votes, not accountability. Blanche was condescending, repeatedly interrupted and tried to gaslight survivors instead of explaining why DOJ released materials that revealed survivors’ identities. He offered no real plan to investigate anyone beyond Epstein and [Ghislaine] Maxwell and gave no reason to believe the remaining files aren’t vital to exposing other perpetrators and enablers.

“Todd Blanche is unfit to be attorney general – Senator Thom Tillis and the Senate must vote no.”

Tillis, however, seemed satisfied, posting: “I commend Todd Blanche for doing what all his predecessors over the last two decades never did: meet with the victims of Jeffrey Epstein’s horrific crimes. I appreciate his willingness to directly engage and listen to them.”

They don’t call Washington the Swamp for nothing. Even after a whole day in the aircon, you feel in need of a shower.

What ancient rite did John Ashcroft, on Thursday a character witness for Todd Blanche, insist on when becoming governor of Missouri, a U.S. senator, and ultimately attorney general under George W. Bush?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

  • 🇵🇸 Don’t forget Gaza:Israeli strikes killed at least five Palestinians, officialssaid, as attacks surged to levels not seen since the ceasefire in name only took effect last October. Meanwhile, Trump’s so-called “Board of Peace” has shrunk plans for rebuilding Gaza to a small pilot project in the south,TheGuardianreported, adding that no substantial progress is expected before Israel’s elections on Oct. 27.

  • 🇺🇦

    Reshuffle kerfuffle:Thousandsprotestedafter President Volodymyr Zelensky replaced his defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, citing friction with Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi, commander of Ukraine’s armed forces. “I’m just showing that if the sides can’t resolve an issue, I will have to resolve it,” Zelenskyy said.

  • 🇬🇧🇦🇷

    Cup of woe:The UKurgedFIFA to investigate after Argentina players displayed a banner claiming sovereignty over the Falkland Islands, or Las Malvinas, after beating England in the World Cup semi-final. “Self-determination rests with the islanders and our commitment to the Falklands will never waver,” Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s spokesperson said.

  • 🇨🇩🇺🇬

    Ebola update:Patients and health workers fled when a hospital in DR Congo wasattackedby a crowd angered by the death of a pregnant woman. Several Ebola patients escaped, officials said. Ugandadischargedits last recorded Ebola patient, triggering a 42-day countdown to be free of the virus.


Missouri Senator Eric Schmitt wants Donald Trump to annex Greenland because… Muslims. Okay.

Ellie Silverman of NOTUS tells the story of Bernita Bowlding, the Black woman seen in a viral picture of masked white supremacists riding the DC Metro. As the picture spread, Bowlding disappeared. When she came back, Silverman writes, she “didn’t know that the photo had gone around the world. She didn’t know she was strong and brave and a symbol of our division and a vessel for our fears. She didn’t know that people who didn’t know her thought they knew her. The icon of America wanted to go now. She needed to pace the parking lot, to sit on the curb, to have some quiet.”

Brad Parscale, the former Trump campaign manager previously seen shirtless and rugby tackled by Florida police, is the subject of a fascinating piece by Eric Cortalessa of Time, about work with online influencers on behalf of Israel, fueling discord on the U.S. right. “Publicly, the campaign was framed as an effort to combat rising antisemitism online,” Cortalessa writes. “An Israeli Foreign Ministry official familiar with the arrangement says there was another strategic aim: preventing young conservatives from turning against Israel.”
